summary: Updated the repository's decomp-toolkit build-template scripts, mostly modernizing Python tooling around build.ninja generation. The PR added type annotations, shifted path handling toward pathlib.Path, centralized Ninja path serialization in tools/ninja_syntax.py, removed project.py's older os_str/unix_str/path helpers, adjusted objdiff JSON dumping to serialize Path objects, fixed a Python error case in upload_progress.py, and expanded .gitignore for generated/tooling artifacts. There was no PR body or review discussion, so motivation is inferred from the title and diff: keep Melee's local DTK template aligned with upstream/tooling expectations and improve cross-platform build script robustness.
what_changed: Added .gitignore entries for .mypy_cache, *.exe, and build, matching generated artifacts introduced or commonly produced by the tooling template.
Added widespread Python type annotations to tooling scripts, including ProjectConfig fields, Object options, helper functions, nested classes, and build/progress generation paths.
Moved Object above ProjectConfig in tools/project.py so ProjectConfig.find_object can return an annotated Optional[Tuple[Dict[str, Any], Object]].
Replaced project.py's local path normalization helpers os_str, unix_str, and path with direct pathlib.Path values passed into ninja_syntax.Writer.

Enhanced tools/ninja_syntax.py with NinjaPath, NinjaPaths, and NinjaPathOrPaths type aliases plus serialize_path/serialize_paths helpers that stringify Path values and normalize backslashes to forward slashes.
Updated Ninja writer APIs to accept strings, Path objects, optional paths, lists of paths, and dict/list variables while filtering None values through serialize_paths.

Changed build.ninja generation call sites to pass Path objects directly for outputs, inputs, implicit deps, depfiles, rspfiles, default targets, and variables instead of wrapping each through path(...).
Made objdiff.json generation store Path objects internally for fields like name, target_path, and base_path, then serialize them via json.dump(..., default=serialize_path).
Added a hard sys.exit("ProjectConfig.compilers_tag missing") path when neither compilers_path nor compilers_tag is set.
Changed upload_progress.py from raising a string to raising KeyError("API key required"), which is valid Python exception behavior.
Added simple annotations to download_tool.py URL builder functions and TOOLS mapping, and to transform_dep.py's import_d_file/main.
smart_moves: Centralizing path conversion in ninja_syntax.serialize_path reduced duplicated path-string logic in project.py and made all Ninja serialization consistently use forward slashes.
Letting build generation pass Path objects until the final Ninja or JSON serialization boundary preserves type information longer and avoids scattered string conversions.
Filtering optional path lists in serialize_paths keeps existing build calls with optional implicit dependencies, such as wrapper_implicit, concise without emitting empty Ninja tokens.
Using json.dump default=serialize_path for objdiff.json avoided manual unix_str conversion at every object field while still producing JSON-compatible path strings.
Replacing a string raise in upload_progress.py with a real exception fixed a latent runtime bug while touching the template.
decomp_lessons: For decomp build templates, prefer pathlib.Path throughout project configuration and build graph assembly, then serialize paths only at file-output boundaries such as build.ninja and objdiff.json.
Ninja generation helpers should own escaping and path normalization; individual configure/project scripts should not duplicate OS-specific path wrappers.
When build rules carry optional implicit dependencies, helper serialization should accept Optional and lists containing Optional values so call sites can remain readable.
Tooling-only template syncs can still affect developer build reproducibility; generated artifacts such as build, .mypy_cache, and downloaded .exe tools should be ignored explicitly.
Type annotations in Python build scripts are useful documentation for decomp project conventions, especially for ProjectConfig, object options, link steps, and progress accounting.
naming_conventions: Introduced NinjaPath, NinjaPaths, and NinjaPathOrPaths aliases in tools/ninja_syntax.py for values accepted by Ninja writer APIs.
Introduced serialize_path and serialize_paths as the replacement naming pattern for path-to-Ninja string conversion.

Kept ProjectConfig as the central configuration object and Object as the per-source/object configuration wrapper with options such as add_to_all, cflags, extra_cflags, mw_version, shiftjis, and source.
Continued using build graph target names such as all_source, all_source_host, dol_diff, dol_apply, apply, and progress_path; this PR did not rename decomp symbols or game code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or C matching changes were present; this PR only changed Python tooling and repo ignore rules.
Indirect matching support improvement: cleaner build.ninja and objdiff.json generation helps maintain reliable compile/link/progress workflows, but no specific function matching tactic was discussed.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice.
The PR body was empty, so there is no recorded reviewer rationale beyond the merged title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching tools/ninja_syntax.py serialize_path or serialize_paths for regressions or refinements.
Search for ProjectConfig.compilers_tag missing to see whether the new hard failure affected downstream configure scripts.
Compare this template update with upstream decomp-toolkit template revisions around January 2024.
Search for later objdiff.json path serialization changes after Path objects were introduced.
Check whether mypy or type checking was later added to CI after .mypy_cache was ignored.
